oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Top 10 things to know about backgrounding nutrition
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Blasi, Dale
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: beef cattle
backgrounding
nutrition
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: The backgrounding sector of the beef cattle production chain bridges the time between weaning and eventual feedlot entry. During this phase, depending upon market weight basis and in­put cost dynamics, calves are typically managed and fed for 45 to 150 days to increase weight, fortify health and prepare them for eventual placement on pasture or direct to the feed yard. Aside from unpredictable health-related input costs during this phase, feed-related costs can represent up to 65% of total costs. A well-managed nutrition program during the background­ing phase can add significant value to the animal by correcting health challenges, conditioning animals for the feedlot, and optimizing feed efficiency at a minimal cost of gain (COG). This paper will outline the top 10 considerations related to back­grounding nutrition that contribute value to the entire beef value chain.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 14-20
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
